text_span: The integration of ferroptosis induction with cancer immunotherapy has emerged as a promising approach in oncology, offering dual mechanisms to overcome therapeutic resistance and tumor heterogeneity.
By inducing lipid peroxidation in tumor tissues, ferroptotic tumor cell death can stimulate immunogenicity.
By inducing lipid peroxidation in tumor tissues, ferroptotic tumor cell death can stimulate immunogenicity.
Ferroptosis, an iron-dependent form of regulated cell death, is characterized by lipid peroxide (LPO) accumulation and suppression of glutathione peroxidase 4 (GPX4) activity.
Ferroptosis, an iron-dependent form of regulated cell death, is characterized by lipid peroxide (LPO) accumulation and suppression of glutathione peroxidase 4 (GPX4) activity.
System Xc−, also known as the cystine/glutamate antiporter, facilitates the cellular uptake of cystine, which is essential for the synthesis of GSH.
IFN-γ downregulates the expression of solute carrier family 3 member 2 (SLC3A2) and solute carrier family 7 member 11 (SLC7A11) on the tumor cell surface through the JAK–STAT1 pathway.

IFN-γ upregulates the expression of long-chain-fatty-acid-CoA ligase 4 (ACSL4), promoting the synthesis of ACSL4-mediated polyunsaturated-fatty-acid-PL (PUFA-PL).
